mechanism_narrative: A rising level of nonfarm payrolls indicates persistent labor demand that supports consumer income and corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Spearman correlation between monthly PAYEMS changes and subsequent 20-day SPX returns is non-positive over a 3-year rolling window, or the strategy Sharpe ratio falls below 0.2.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the federal funds rate exceeding 0.05 percentage points marks the onset of an active tightening cycle, raising discount rates and debt-service costs, which drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In triggered months, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances, or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: The multiplicative interaction between the 3-month percent change in CPIAUCSL and the level of FEDFUNDS captures the compression of real policy rates; when inflation accelerates while nominal rates remain near zero, the interaction term spikes and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days due to an impending violent monetary repricing.
failure_condition: The interaction term (CPIAUCSL 3-month change times FEDFUNDS level) shows a positive or zero rank correlation with 60-day SPX returns over any 2-year rolling window, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in a pooled regression.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a quarter-over-quarter annualized GDP growth rate below 2% signals demand exhaustion and impending earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When GDPC1 growth is below 2% during late-cycle months, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of cases, or the information coefficient is above zero.

mechanism_narrative: A rising industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which lifts corporate operating leverage and profitability, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Monthly changes in INDPRO fail to positively predict 5-day SPX returns with a t-statistic greater than 1.5 over a 3-year rolling window, or directional accuracy falls below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in housing starts exceeding 5 percent foreshadows a contraction in residential investment and downstream consumer durable demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Following triggers, 20-day SPX returns are non-negative on average (t-test p > 0.05) or the hit rate of negative returns is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction between the 3-month change in nonfarm payrolls and the level of the unemployment rate captures labor market tightness; when payroll growth is positive while unemployment is already below 4%, wage pressure compresses profit margins and expands equity risk premiums, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term (PAYEMS 3-month change times UNRATE level) exhibits a positive rank correlation with 5-day SPX returns over any 2-year rolling window, or the conditional 5-day return is not negative when unemployment is below 4%.

mechanism_narrative: A negative 3-month change in retail sales signals a sustained loss of consumer momentum that translates into revenue misses across the retail and consumer discretionary sectors, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When RSAFS declines over 3 months, the subsequent 60-day SPX return is non-negative on average (t-test p > 0.10) or the frequency of negative returns is less than 50%.
